columns.name: person_id
columns.description: unique identifier that joins this table to other tables

columns.name: gender_source_value
columns.description: gender of patient: 'M' for male, 'F' for female

columns.name: year_of_birth
columns.description: year the patient was born. To filter by age, use: YEAR(CURRENT_DATE) - year_of_birth >= <age>. To filter by diagnosis year, use condition_start_date instead.

columns.name: race_source_value
columns.description: race/ethnicity of patient as a text label. Values include: 'Chinese', 'Malay', 'Indian', 'Others'. Use LIKE or = to filter.

condition_occurrence.description: Condition of patient — one row per cancer diagnosis

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: condition_occurrence_id
columns.description: primary key (e.g. 'dx56418')

columns.name: condition_start_date
columns.description: date of diagnosis. When filtering for a specific year, always generate TWO filters: one with >= 'YYYY-01-01' and one with <= 'YYYY-12-31'. Only use a single bound for filtering before or after a certain year.

columns.name: condition_source_value
columns.description: condition stored as: topo_code||topo_description||histo_code||histo_description. e.g. 'C18.3||Hepatic flexure||8140.3||Adenocarcinoma'. Prefer filtering on ICD10 or ICDO3 columns for site/histology. If filtering this column, use op='or_like' with value=["%C18%", "%C19%", "%C20%"] for all colorectal cancer. Do NOT filter using generic labels like 'colorectal cancer'.

columns.name: ICD10
columns.description: ICD-10 topography (site) code. Use this column to filter cancer site. For all colorectal cancer use op='in' with value=['C18.0','C18.1','C18.2','C18.3','C18.4','C18.5','C18.6','C18.7','C18.8','C18.9','C19','C20']. For colon only use op='in' with C18.x and C19 codes. For rectum use op='=' with value='C20'. For a specific sub-site use op='=' with the exact code.

columns.name: ICDO3
columns.description: ICD-O-3 morphology (histology) code. Use this column to filter tumour type. Common values: '8140/3' (adenocarcinoma NOS), '8480/3' (mucinous adenocarcinoma), '8490/3' (signet ring cell carcinoma), '8263/3' (tubulovillous adenoma). Use op='like' e.g. '%8140%' to match a histology family.

procedure_occurrence.description: Surgery and radiotherapy procedures. Surgical rows have procedure_concept_id=NULL; radiotherapy rows have procedure_concept_id set.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: procedure_occurrence_id
columns.description: primary key. Surgical rows use prefix 'procedure_sx_'; radiotherapy rows use 'procedure_rt_'.

columns.name: institution
columns.description: hospital/institution where the procedure was performed. Values: 'SGH', 'NCCS', 'CGH', 'SKH', 'KKH', 'NHCS'.

columns.name: procedure_date
columns.description: date of the procedure in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use this for date filtering (preferred over procedure_datetime for date comparisons).

columns.name: procedure_datetime
columns.description: start date and time of procedure in DD/MM/YYYY HH:MM format.

columns.name: procedure_end_date
columns.description: end date of the procedure in YYYY-MM-DD format.

columns.name: procedure_end_datetime
columns.description: end date and time of procedure.

columns.name: procedure_concept_id
columns.description: concept ID of procedure. NULL for surgical procedures; non-NULL for radiotherapy.

columns.name: procedure_source_value
columns.description: free-text description of the surgery or radiotherapy technique.

columns.name: TOSP
columns.description: Table of Surgical Procedures code used for billing classification in Singapore. e.g. 'SB805A'. Use LIKE to filter by procedure category.

columns.name: intent
columns.description: treatment intent for radiotherapy rows. Values: 'Curative', 'Palliative', 'Adjuvant'. NULL for surgical rows.

columns.name: delivered_dose
columns.description: total radiation dose delivered in Gray (Gy). Only populated for radiotherapy rows.

columns.name: delivered_fraction
columns.description: number of radiotherapy fractions delivered. Only populated for radiotherapy rows.

measurement_mutation.description: EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) table storing staging, mutation, grade, and laterality measurements. Each row is one measurement type for one patient encounter. Always filter measurement_concept_name to select the measurement type.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: measurement_id
columns.description: primary key (e.g. 'meas_00123')

columns.name: measurement_concept_name
columns.description: type of measurement — ALWAYS add a WHERE filter on this column to restrict to the relevant type. Use these exact values: for clinical staging use 'TNM Clin Stage Group'; for pathologic staging use 'TNM Path Stage Group'; for T/N/M components use 'TNM Clin T', 'TNM Clin N', 'TNM Clin M', 'TNM Path T', 'TNM Path N', 'TNM Path M'; for mutations use 'BRAF Mutation Conclusion', 'KRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'NRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'APC Mutation Conclusion'; for grade use 'Grade'; for laterality use 'Laterality'. If the question mentions stage without specifying clinical or pathologic, filter with IN ('TNM Clin Stage Group', 'TNM Path Stage Group'). When the question asks about multiple mutations together (e.g. KRAS, BRAF, NRAS) always use op='in' with value as a list e.g. ['KRAS Mutation Conclusion', 'BRAF Mutation Conclusion', 'NRAS Mutation Conclusion']. Never use op='=' when multiple mutations are requested. When measurement_concept_name is filtered to a mutation type, always also filter value_as_concept_name = 'mutation detected' unless the question asks for both detected and not detected. When measurement_concept_name is filtered to a stage type, filter value_as_concept_name using op='in' with the relevant stage values e.g. ['I', 'II', 'IIA', 'IIB', 'IIC'] for early stage or ['III', 'IIIA', 'IIIB', 'IIIC', 'IVA', 'IVB', 'IVC'] for advanced stage.

columns.name: measurement_date
columns.description: date the measurement was recorded. Use for time-based filtering of mutation or staging results.

columns.name: value_as_concept_name
columns.description: result of the measurement. Mutation values: 'mutation detected', 'no mutation detected'. Grade values: 'Grade I', 'Grade II', 'Grade III', 'Grade/differentiation unknown, not stated, or not applicable'. Stage group values: 'I', 'II', 'IIA', 'IIB', 'IIC', 'III', 'IIIA', 'IIIB', 'IIIC', 'IVA', 'IVB', 'IVC', 'Stage Unknown'. Laterality values: 'Not a paired site'.

columns.name: value_source_value
columns.description: raw source value of the measurement result before standardisation. Useful for free-text or non-standardised values.

columns.name: measurement_event_id
columns.description: foreign key linking to procedure_occurrence_id of the associated procedure encounter

death.description: Death records. Always LEFT JOIN so living patients (no death record) are not excluded from counts.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: death_date
columns.description: date of death. NULL means patient is still alive (or status unknown). To filter for deceased patients use: death_date IS NOT NULL.

columns.name: cause_source_value
columns.description: cause of death as recorded in source data.

drug_exposure_cancerdrugs.description: Cancer drug/chemotherapy treatments. One row per drug prescription episode.

columns.name: person_id
columns.description: use to join to person

columns.name: drug_exposure_id
columns.description: primary key

columns.name: drug_source_value
columns.description: prescribed drug identifier: NDC code || Gemscript code. Use drug_source_value or ATC for drug filtering.

columns.name: ATC
columns.description: ATC (Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical) classification code for the drug. Use this for filtering by drug class or therapeutic category (e.g. 'L01' for antineoplastic agents). Use op='like' e.g. '%L01%' to match a drug class.

columns.name: drug_strength
columns.description: dose strength of the drug as prescribed (e.g. '500mg').

columns.name: drug_form
columns.description: formulation/dosage form of the drug (e.g. 'Tablet', 'Injection', 'Infusion').

columns.name: route_source_value
columns.description: route of administration (e.g. 'Oral', 'Intravenous', 'Subcutaneous').

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: quantity of drug dispensed per prescription episode.

columns.name: case_type
columns.description: clinical case type for the drug episode (e.g. 'Inpatient', 'Outpatient', 'Day Surgery').

columns.name: drug_exposure_start_date
columns.description: start date of the drug prescription/administration.

columns.name: drug_exposure_end_date
columns.description: end date of the drug prescription/administration.

columns.name: institution
columns.description: hospital/institution where the drug was prescribed. Values: 'SGH', 'NCCS', 'CGH', 'SKH', 'KKH', 'NHCS'.
